metadata.title: Discovery Engine Dataset: Computational Identification of Geroprotective Compound Candidates via Multi-Source Biological Network Analysis

creators.name: Azevedo, Tercio S.
creators.affiliation: Independent Researcher, Sao Carlos, SP, Brazil

metadata.description:
This dataset accompanies the paper "Computational Identification of Geroprotective Compound Candidates via Multi-Source Biological Network Analysis". It contains all processed data, validation results, and figures from the Discovery Engine project.

The Discovery Engine pipeline integrates 6 public biological databases (PubMed, Semantic Scholar, GenAge, Open Targets, ChEMBL, DrugAge) into a knowledge graph (479 nodes, 898 edges) and ranks 162 drug candidates for geroprotective potential using a weighted multi-feature scoring system.

Key results:

  • Rapamycin (rank #1) and Metformin (rank #10) correctly identified as top geroprotectors
  • External validation: 4.42x enrichment of DrugAge compounds in top-20 (Mann-Whitney p=1.59e-3)
  • Pathway enrichment: 11/11 aging pathway categories significantly enriched (p=1.27e-20)
  • Novel candidates: Bezafibrate (PPAR agonist, +13% lifespan), Bardoxolone methyl (NRF2 activator), Venetoclax (BCL-2 senolytic)

Pipeline code: github.com/terciosilas/discovery-engine
metadata.notes: Pipeline deterministic with seed=42. All inputs/outputs tracked via SHA-256 checksums. 106 automated tests ensure correctness. Computational assistance provided by Claude Code (Anthropic).
